message:
fix(sync): make try_lock yield to queued waiters instead of stealing the lock

The previous FIFO fairness fix (a147bc2d) prevented lock steal from
breaking waiter ordering during the poll path, but try_lock() could
still acquire the lock when waiters were queued. This violated the
fairness guarantee because a non-async caller could bypass the entire
waiter queue.

Strengthen the guarantee: try_lock() now returns Err(TryLockError::Locked)
whenever the waiter queue is non-empty, regardless of whether the lock
itself is free. This applies to both Mutex::try_lock() and
OwnedMutexGuard::try_lock(). The TryLockError::Locked doc is updated
to reflect that it means "unavailable" (locked or queued waiters exist).

Rename tests to reflect the new semantics:
- try_lock_steal_does_not_lose_waiter → try_lock_does_not_bypass_queued_waiter
- try_lock_steal_with_two_waiters_preserves_fifo → try_lock_with_two_waiters_preserves_fifo

Add new test owned_mutex_try_lock_blocked_by_queued_waiter that verifies
the OwnedMutexGuard path also respects queued waiters.

Update tests/lost_wakeup_bug.rs assertions: unlock() now wakes the
waiter but does not remove it from the queue (it stays queued until it
acquires or drops), so the waiter count after unlock is 3 not 2, with
comments updated to match the new queue lifecycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
